summary: In mass spectrometry imaging, adducts can form between target molecules such as metabolites and other substances such as matrix or salt ions.
An adduct refers specifically to a transformation caused by the addition of a chemical moiety, often during the MSI procedure.
The precursor to an adduct is called the parent ion or parent molecule.
The mass2adduct analysis looks at pairs of peaks and attempts to match their massdiffs to known chemical transformations to find adducts derived from the chemical matrix used for MALDI-MSI.
The massdiff function returns an object of classes data.frame and massdiff.
In the massdiff output, column A represents the parent ion peak mass. In the massdiff output, column B represents the putative adduct ion peak mass (higher than A).
In the massdiff output, column diff is the difference between A and B.
adductMatch can be applied to a massdiff object to add a matches column listing the closest-matching adduct for each ion pair.
The object produced by adductMatch is a subset of the original massdiff object, as ion pairs without a matching adduct are excluded.
corrPairsMSI performs a correlation test on each pair of peaks in a massdiff object.
corrPairsMSI uses the two-tailed Pearson method by default for correlation testing.
Correlation test results in corrPairsMSI output include Estimate (correlation coefficient), P.value, and Significance.
The Significance column in corrPairsMSI output indicates whether the p-value is below the threshold after Bonferroni correction.
The pointsAdducts function annotates the original mass spectrum using a massdiff object to mark peaks in different colors.
Some adduct ions may themselves be parents of further adducts.
